summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a detailed presentation on digital signage options and locations along State Route 28, approved several personnel and training motions, selected a new Employee Assistance Program provider, accepted a firefighter's retirement, and discussed zoning changes including agricultural district designations and accessory structure regulations. The board also heard communications including commendation letters for police and zoning staff, and updates on EMS billing revenue increases.
decisions: Approved payment of bills from January 24 through February 13, 2023
Approved motion to amend 12/4/23 motion correcting EMS revenue line item for AEDs (2193.8920000)
Approved appropriation of $43.49 to line item 2251.210.1120000 for police salaries to close out federal account
Approved SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend Ohio School Resource Officers Conference in Sandusky ($640 per officer)
Approved SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend National School Resource Officer Conference in Indianapolis ($600 per officer)
Approved buyback of two cemetery graves (Section A, Block 342, Graves 1 and 3) at $500 each totaling $1,000
Approved The Standard Option 2 as new Employee Assistance Program provider at $0.60 per employee per month effective March 1
Accepted retirement of part-time firefighter William (Bill) Waits effective 2-20-2023
Approved Chief Myers to attend Andy Frederick's Training Days in Lexington, KY March 20-22 (not to exceed $550)
action_items: Chief Rose to conduct plywood visibility test and investigate sign placement between utility poles near State Route 28/Goshen Road, including Duke Energy electric meter and ODOT coordination
Justin Scaff (One Stop Signs) to provide updated design options and pricing for gateway sign at Deerfield Road by February 28 meeting
Harry Holbert to work with zoning on lot consolidation and conditional use for digital sign location
Chief Rose to investigate electric service options (independent meter vs directional bore) for sign location
Chief Myers to present Station 18 building findings at next meeting
Harry Holbert to reach out to OSU Extension in Owensville regarding agricultural zoning designations
Harry Holbert to follow up with prosecutor's office (Julia) on zoning legal questions
Board to schedule another zoning workshop for accessory structures and agricultural district discussions
Chad (service department) to plan landscaping for gateway sign location once installed
key_amounts: $39,646 increase in annual EMS billing collections
$330 per call EMS billing collection rate (up $6.35 from $324)
84 additional EMS transports over prior year
$47,950 for digital sign from One Stop Signs/NextLED
$8,500 original estimate for secondary gateway sign (to be reduced with design changes)
$400 engineering cost for sealed sign drawings
$43.49 appropriated to close out federal police account
$640 per officer registration for Ohio SRO Conference
$600 per officer registration for National SRO Conference
$1,000 total for cemetery grave buyback ($500 each)
$0.60 per employee per month for Standard EAP Option 2 ($55.80/month for 93 employees, ~$672/year)
$0.35 per employee per month for Standard EAP Option 1 ($32.55/month)
$3,000 per year flat rate for Medical Mutual EAP alternative
$95 per month previously paid for Anthem EAP
$550 not-to-exceed for Chief Myers training in Lexington, KY
188 new permits received from Village of Belmont addressing
notable_topics: Digital township signage - detailed presentation on locations, size, visibility, and costs for LED sign on State Route 28
Gateway sign for Goshen Township at Deerfield Road/State Route 28 entrance
Employee Assistance Program provider selection (replacing Anthem with The Standard)
Agricultural zoning designations (AG-1 and AG-2 proposals for farmland protection)
Accessory structure zoning regulations and variance concerns
Police department CALEA accreditation process underway
Retirement of part-time firefighter Bill Waits
SRO conference training approvals for school resource officers
Local Government Fund advocacy through Clermont County Township Association
American Regulators selected as Salute to Leaders honoree (March 29 at the Oasis)
Village of Belmont development and State Route 28 access improvements
EMS billing revenue increase report from Medi-Count
notes: Trustee Allen was absent; board expressed desire to include her in final digital sign vote. Transcript was truncated before meeting concluded, so additional agenda items and motions may have occurred. The digital sign discussion was extensive with seven location options evaluated; the board favored location #6 near State Route 28 and Goshen Road on township property. The $50,000 bidding threshold was a concern as combined sign costs could exceed it. Police CALEA accreditation was noted as ongoing. The zoning discussion revealed frustration about pace of progress on agricultural and accessory structure code changes.
title: BOT Meeting 2.14.23.mp4
